supersedes: the removed openclaw-agent-project vault-map.json + aggregate-mount runtime-secret model
summary: Authoritative per-agent 1Password vault authorization for the deployed concern-lane system, scoped to the operator's five concerns: Azabu corporate + azabu.io (★1), foxcale customer work (★2), Cisco partner-SE, self-study, and personal. Each agent is granted only the vault(s) its concern requires. Authorization is enforced at the host boundary by per-agent runtime-secret snapshots (materialize-runtime-secrets.js), not by prompt-level policy text, so a prompt-injected or misbehaving agent cannot read another concern's credentials.

principles.least_privilege: Grant each agent the minimum vault set for its concern; do not grant access to more vaults than needed.
principles.complete_mediation: Enforce credential isolation in the runtime (per-agent secret snapshot + per-agent read-only bind mount), never by asking the model to honor a must-not-read rule.
principles.zero_trust: Treat every lane as untrusted regardless of being on the same host; scope credentials per agent and per session.
principles.concern_isolation: ★1 (Azabu, azabu.io) and ★2 (foxcale) use distinct GitHub tokens in distinct vaults and must never mix: no agent holds both, and no grant falls back across the two. Cisco partner-SE work (work-cisco) holds no Azabu/foxcale vault and no GitHub credential.
sources: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications LLM06:2025 Excessive Agency — minimize extension permissions; execute extensions in the user's security scope with minimum privileges; implement authorization in downstream systems (complete mediation). https://genai.owasp.org/llmrisk/llm062025-excessive-agency/
NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust Architecture — least-privilege, per-session/per-request access; all communication secured regardless of network location. https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/207/final
1Password CLI / service-account best practices — use dedicated vaults that are properly scoped; do not grant access to more vaults than needed. https://www.1password.dev/cli/best-practices/

router-agent.note: User-facing orchestrator. Delegates work to concern agents; must never hold a concern's domain credentials.

telegram-fable.note: Workspace-artifact builder. Needs gateway/preview config from its own vault; no GitHub credentials.

azabu-corporate.concern: ★1 Azabu corporate operations + azabu.io site maintenance
azabu-corporate.note: Holds the Azabu GitHub token (GITHUB_TOKEN) for azabu.io repo work. Must never hold or read the foxcale vault.

foxcale-coding.note: Holds the foxcale project GitHub token (GITHUB_PAT_F_PROJECT). Must never hold or read the Azabu vault.

work-cisco.note: No GitHub credential and no Azabu/foxcale vault — Cisco work carries no Azabu element.

personal.note: Personal life admin. No GitHub credential.

runtime_secret_grants.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
runtime_secret_grants.purpose: ★1 Azabu GitHub access for azabu.io repository / PR work (Azabu token only)

runtime_secret_grants.name: GITHUB_PAT_F_PROJECT
runtime_secret_grants.purpose: ★2 foxcale project GitHub access (foxcale token only)

vaults_to_create.reason: Optional dedicated vault for foxcale advisory tooling credentials (no GitHub token).
Optional dedicated vault for learning/reference API keys.
Optional dedicated vault for personal-service credentials.
Optional dedicated vault for artifact gateway/preview credentials.
